file_insights.purpose: Centralized configuration constants for E-Menum application

patterns_discovered.description: Uses TypeScript type system to define valid keys (PlanType, FeatureKey, etc.) and validates records against these types using satisfies operator
Subscription plans organized with position, pricing, and feature availability matrix across all plan tiers
Uses -1 value to represent unlimited resources in tier limits (enterprise plan)
Complex configuration organized into logical sections (resources, monthly, validation, upload, etc.)
ORDER_STATUS_TRANSITIONS defines valid state transitions as a lookup table
VALIDATION object groups constraints by entity type (name.menu, name.product, etc.)
AI_CREDIT_COSTS maps operation types to numeric costs for usage tracking

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff output was truncated at 21189 characters, full file content not visible in provided diff
Constants like ORDER_AUTO_COMPLETE_DELAY_MS use formula (30 * 60 * 1000) which could benefit from named intermediate constants
Monthly prices stored as integers (2000, 4000, etc.) - currency unit not specified, could be confusing without documentation
Some features are available in free tier (digitalMenu, qrCodeGeneration) while others have restrictions, pattern not immediately obvious
aiCredits is the only monthly limit with a non-unlimited value (10000) for enterprise tier, deviates from pattern

approach_outcome.notes: Constants file successfully created with comprehensive configuration for subscription tiers, feature flags, AI costs, validation rules, and order management

recommendations.recommendation: Add currency unit clarification (e.g., 'priceMonthly in cents' or 'priceMonthly in Turkish Lira') to PLANS object documentation
Extract time calculation constants (e.g., const THIRTY_MINUTES_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000) to reduce magic numbers
Consider using branded types or nominal typing for numeric values like prices and AI credits to prevent accidental unit confusion
Add validation rules for URL/domain constraints (custom domain length, format) given customDomain feature availability
Consider extracting plan-specific configurations into separate objects if complexity grows beyond current 5 tiers
Document why enterprise aiCredits is 10000 (capped) rather than -1 (unlimited) as this deviates from other monthly limits pattern
